name: Patricia Ong

patient.name: Patricia Ong
patient.chief_complaint: Fatigue, reduced appetite, mild forgetfulness; medication name uncertain
medications: unknown evening tablet (caregiver unsure of name)
patient.living_situation: Lives alone in a walk-up flat; niece Denise visits thrice weekly

conversation_text:
Care coordinator: Denise, thanks for joining. How is Patricia day to day?
Caregiver (Denise): Auntie Pat is seventy-six. She's exhausted by late morning and often needs a rest before going to the market. Her thighs feel weak on the stairs; she holds the rail with both hands now. Appetite is down — she nibbles toast and skips dinner twice a week — and I think she's thinner in the face. Memory-wise she forgot our Tuesday video call and asked the same question about the air-con remote twice. Medications: she takes her thyroid pill every morning, that one's clear, but there's a small white tablet she takes at night for 'nerves or sleep or something' — I honestly cannot remember the name, and the blister pack is from a private clinic without an English label I can read. Please don't guess it for me; I just don't know. No falls that I'm aware of.
